Profile Photo

Add Your Photo to the Website

📷
Click here to preview your photo
(for preview only — see instructions to add permanently)
Dr. Aqib Raza Jillani PT
Doctor of Physical Therapy · DPT
Click to change photo
✅ AHPC 🏛️ PPTA 🇺🇸 APTA 🌍 WHO/UN 🇬🇧 OpenLearn
1
Choose the Right Photo
Use a professional photo — clear face, good lighting, plain or clinic background. A portrait (3:4 ratio) works best. Minimum 600×800px recommended for sharpness.
2
Save the Photo as dr-aqib.jpg
Rename your photo file to exactly dr-aqib.jpg on your computer. This name is already referenced in the HTML code — no code changes needed.
3
Place in the Same Folder as the HTML
Put dr-aqib.jpg in the exact same folder/directory as your index.html file. They must be side-by-side in the same location.
4
Open the HTML and the Photo Appears!
Open index.html in any browser. Your photo will automatically appear in the About section and hero sidebar — no coding required.
5
Hosting on a Website? Upload Both Files
If you're uploading to a hosting service (like cPanel, Hostinger, or Namecheap), upload both index.html AND dr-aqib.jpg to the same folder on your server.
📸 Best Photo Tips
  • Wear your white coat / lab coat for a professional medical look
  • Good front lighting — avoid harsh shadows on face
  • Plain white, teal, or dark background works best with this design
  • Square or portrait orientation — landscape won't fit well
  • File size under 500KB for fast loading (compress at tinypng.com)
  • JPG format is preferred — PNG also works
// WHERE THE PHOTO IS REFERENCED IN CODE

About Me

Your Trusted Physiotherapist

AR
Dr. Aqib Raza Jillani PT
Doctor of Physical Therapy · DPT
✅ AHPC 🏛️ PPTA 🇺🇸 APTA 🌍 WHO/UN 🇬🇧 OpenLearn

I'm Dr. Aqib Raza Jillani PT — an AHPC Registered Physiotherapist with a Doctor of Physical Therapy (DPT) degree, recognized by the Higher Education Commission (HEC) of Pakistan. I offer in-clinic and telehealth consultations nationwide and internationally — contact me directly via phone or WhatsApp to book.

With 8+ years of progressive clinical experience across leading institutions including Shaikh Zayed Hospital, Lahore, DHQ Teaching Hospital, Sargodha, and City Physical Therapy, Islamabad, I specialize in orthopedic, neurological, musculoskeletal, sports, paediatric, and cardiopulmonary rehabilitation — always guided by evidence-based, patient-centered principles.

My international certifications include the American Physical Therapy Association (APTA), World Health Organization (WHO), the United Nations Agora Platform, and The Open University UK — reflecting my commitment to globally informed, continuously updated clinical practice.

"Health is Wealth — and I'm here to protect yours. Let me explain your condition, your treatment, and your recovery — clearly, completely, and with care."

Follow along as @drjillanicares on all social platforms for free physiotherapy education in Urdu and English every week. Whether you visit the clinic or connect online, I'm here to help you move better and live better.

💬 WhatsApp Me View Services →

Professional History

Clinical Experience

DHQ Teaching Hospital

What I Treat

Clinical Services

🦴
Orthopedic Rehabilitation
Fractures, ligament injuries, joint replacements, post-surgical rehab. Goal-focused recovery to restore full strength and function.
Clinic · Telehealth Available
🧠
Neurological Rehabilitation
Stroke, spinal cord injuries, cerebral palsy, TBI — restoring independence and quality of life with structured neurological programs.
Clinic · Home Visit Option
🏃
Sports Injury Rehabilitation
Evidence-based treatment for sports injuries. Custom return-to-sport programs for athletes of all levels — amateur to competitive.
Clinic · Performance Focus
✋
Manual Therapy
Advanced hands-on joint mobilization, manipulation, and soft tissue therapy to restore mobility, reduce pain, and improve function.
Clinic Only
⚡
Electrotherapy
TENS, therapeutic ultrasound, IFT, and laser therapy — modern modalities for effective pain relief and accelerated tissue healing.
Clinic Only
💊
Pain Management
Drug-free holistic pain management for chronic back pain, neck pain, knee osteoarthritis. Treat the root cause — not just symptoms.
Clinic · Telehealth Available
🧒
Paediatric Physiotherapy
Child-centered, family-involved rehabilitation for developmental delays, cerebral palsy, torticollis, and neurological conditions.
Clinic · Family Guided
❤️
Cardiopulmonary Rehab
Structured programs for cardiac and pulmonary patients — improving exercise tolerance, breathing mechanics, and overall quality of life.
Clinic · Supervised
🌐
Telehealth Consultation
Online assessment, home exercise program design, and follow-up sessions — accessible from anywhere in Pakistan and internationally.
Online · Nationwide
Free Health Education

Physiotherapy Tips & Advice

Quick, expert-backed tips from Dr. Aqib Raza Jillani PT — apply these at home today. For personalized advice, book a consultation.

🔴
Chronic Back Pain
  • Avoid sitting for more than 45 minutes at a stretch
  • Sleep on your side with a pillow between your knees
  • Strengthen your core muscles — they protect your spine
  • Never lift heavy objects with a bent back
  • Heat therapy (warm pad) helps muscle spasm relief
→ Book for Back Pain Treatment
🟣
Neck Pain & Cervical
  • Keep your screen at eye level — not below
  • Take a phone break every 20 minutes (text neck)
  • Do gentle neck stretches morning and evening
  • Use a cervical pillow for proper neck support while sleeping
  • Avoid crunching your neck down over your phone
→ Book for Neck Pain Treatment
🟡
Knee Pain & Arthritis
  • Strengthen your quadriceps — they support the knee joint
  • Avoid climbing stairs repeatedly when in acute pain
  • Cold pack (ice) helps swelling, heat helps stiffness
  • Walking on flat surfaces is better than hills
  • Maintain healthy weight — every kg reduces 4x knee load
→ Book for Knee Pain Treatment
🟢
Posture Correction
  • Sit with your back fully supported against your chair
  • Keep your feet flat on the floor — not crossed
  • Shoulder blades should be gently back and down
  • Your ear should be above your shoulder, not in front
  • Stand tall — imagine a string pulling your head upward
→ Book Posture Assessment
🩷
Frozen Shoulder
  • Never force a painful movement — work in pain-free range
  • Pendulum exercises daily help maintain joint mobility
  • Warm up shoulder before any stretching (warm towel)
  • Sleep on the non-affected side with support under arm
  • Early treatment gives best recovery — don't delay!
→ Book for Shoulder Treatment
💚
General Exercise & Wellness
  • 30 minutes of walking daily prevents most musculoskeletal problems
  • Stretching in the morning reduces daily aches and stiffness
  • Drink 8+ glasses of water — joints need hydration
  • Exercise is medicine — movement is the best painkiller
  • Consistency beats intensity — daily light activity > weekly hard gym
→ Watch Free Exercise Videos

Common Questions

Frequently Asked Questions

Do I need a doctor's referral to see a physiotherapist?+
No, you do not need a referral in most cases. You can directly book an appointment with Dr. Aqib via WhatsApp or by visiting the clinic. However, if you have recent X-rays, MRI reports, or doctor notes, please bring them as they help with assessment.
How does a Telehealth / Online consultation work?+
Book via WhatsApp at +92 304 843 8337. A video or call session is arranged at a convenient time. Dr. Aqib conducts a full assessment, discusses your condition, and provides a personalized home exercise program — all online. Available for patients across Pakistan and internationally.
What should I bring to my first clinic appointment?+
Please bring any relevant medical documents such as X-rays, MRI scans, blood reports, previous prescriptions, or doctor's notes. Wear comfortable clothing that allows easy access to the area being treated. Arrive 5–10 minutes early to complete a brief patient form.
How many sessions will I need?+
This depends on your condition, severity, and individual response to treatment. After your initial assessment, Dr. Aqib will give you an estimated treatment timeline and session plan. Acute conditions may resolve in 4–8 sessions, while chronic or neurological conditions may require longer programs.
Is physiotherapy painful?+
Some techniques such as deep tissue massage or joint mobilization may cause mild discomfort, but physiotherapy should never be severely painful. Dr. Aqib always works within your comfort level and adjusts treatment based on your feedback every session.
Can physiotherapy help after surgery?+
Yes — post-surgical rehabilitation is one of the most important aspects of recovery. Physiotherapy helps restore movement, strength, and function after orthopedic surgeries such as knee replacement, ACL repair, spinal surgery, shoulder surgery, and more. Early intervention leads to faster and better outcomes.
Do you treat children?+
Yes. Dr. Aqib provides paediatric physiotherapy for children with developmental delays, cerebral palsy, torticollis, neurological conditions, and more. Sessions are child-friendly and family-involved to ensure the best outcomes.
How do I book an appointment?+
The easiest way is via WhatsApp at +92 304 843 8337. You can also fill out the booking form on this website or send an email to drjillanicares@gmail.com. The clinic address in Sillanwali, Sargodha will be confirmed upon booking (Mon–Fri, 10 AM – 4 PM).
